verdict.answer_first: A states the answer in a scannable table immediately: "| Guarantee | Question it answers | Mechanism |"
verdict.decisiveness: B provides a conclusive, bolded summary sentence at the end, whereas A ends with a question: "**TLS authenticates the server with certificates, agrees on a secret nobody can overhear using Diffie-Hellman...**"
verdict.actionability: B is more actionable because its section 'Why this resists the obvious attacks' directly maps the technical mechanisms to the security outcomes a developer cares about.
